001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.SystemUtils.*;
023
024import java.io.*;
025import java.lang.reflect.*;
026import java.nio.charset.*;
027import java.util.*;
028
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.http.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.*;
034import org.apache.juneau.marshall.html.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.marshall.msgpack.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.marshall.objecttools.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.marshall.stream.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.marshall.swap.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.marshall.uon.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.marshall.xml.*;
042
043/**
044 * Parent class for all Juneau parsers.
045 *
046 * <h5 class='topic'>Valid data conversions</h5>
047 * <p>
048 * Parsers can parse any parsable POJO types, as specified in the <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/PojoCategories">POJO Categories</a>.
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * Some examples of conversions are shown below...
052 * </p>
053 * <table class='styled'>
054 *    <tr>
055 *       <th>Data type</th>
056 *       <th>Class type</th>
057 *       <th>JSON example</th>
058 *       <th>XML example</th>
059 *       <th>Class examples</th>
060 *    </tr>
061 *    <tr>
062 *       <td>object</td>
063 *       <td>Maps, Java beans</td>
064 *       <td class='code'>{name:<js>'John Smith'</js>,age:21}</td>
065 *       <td class='code'><xt>&lt;object&gt;
066 *    &lt;name</xt> <xa>type</xa>=<xs>'string'</xs><xt>&gt;</xt>John Smith<xt>&lt;/name&gt;
067 *    &lt;age</xt> <xa>type</xa>=<xs>'number'</xs><xt>&gt;</xt>21<xt>&lt;/age&gt;
068 * &lt;/object&gt;</xt></td>
069 *       <td class='code'>HashMap, TreeMap&lt;String,Integer&gt;</td>
070 *    </tr>
071 *    <tr>
072 *       <td>array</td>
073 *       <td>Collections, Java arrays</td>
074 *       <td class='code'>[1,2,3]</td>
075 *       <td class='code'><xt>&lt;array&gt;
076 *    &lt;number&gt;</xt>1<xt>&lt;/number&gt;
077 *    &lt;number&gt;</xt>2<xt>&lt;/number&gt;
078 *    &lt;number&gt;</xt>3<xt>&lt;/number&gt;
079 * &lt;/array&gt;</xt></td>
080 *       <td class='code'>List&lt;Integer&gt;, <jk>int</jk>[], Float[], Set&lt;Person&gt;</td>
081 *    </tr>
082 *    <tr>
083 *       <td>number</td>
084 *       <td>Numbers</td>
085 *       <td class='code'>123</td>
086 *       <td class='code'><xt>&lt;number&gt;</xt>123<xt>&lt;/number&gt;</xt></td>
087 *       <td class='code'>Integer, Long, Float, <jk>int</jk></td>
088 *    </tr>
089 *    <tr>
090 *       <td>boolean</td>
091 *       <td>Booleans</td>
092 *       <td class='code'><jk>true</jk></td>
093 *       <td class='code'><xt>&lt;boolean&gt;</xt>true<xt>&lt;/boolean&gt;</xt></td>
094 *       <td class='code'>Boolean</td>
095 *    </tr>
096 *    <tr>
097 *       <td>string</td>
098 *       <td>CharSequences</td>
099 *       <td class='code'><js>'foobar'</js></td>
100 *       <td class='code'><xt>&lt;string&gt;</xt>foobar<xt>&lt;/string&gt;</xt></td>
101 *       <td class='code'>String, StringBuilder</td>
102 *    </tr>
103 * </table>
104 *
105 * <p>
106 * In addition, any class types with {@link ObjectSwap ObjectSwaps} associated with them on the registered
107 * bean context can also be passed in.
108 *
109 * <p>
110 * For example, if a {@link CalendarFormat}-driven swap is used to generalize {@code Calendar} objects to {@code String}
111 * objects.
112 * When registered with this parser, you can construct {@code Calendar} objects from {@code Strings} using the
113 * following syntax...
114 * <p class='bjava'>
115 *    Calendar <jv>calendar</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<js>"'Sun Mar 03 04:05:06 EST 2001'"</js>, GregorianCalendar.<jk>class</jk>);
116 * </p>
117 *
118 * <p>
119 * If <code>Object.<jk>class</jk></code> is specified as the target type, then the parser automatically determines the
120 * data types and generates the following object types...
121 * <table class='styled'>
122 *    <tr><th>JSON type</th><th>Class type</th></tr>
123 *    <tr><td>object</td><td>{@link JsonMap}</td></tr>
124 *    <tr><td>array</td><td>{@link JsonList}</td></tr>
125 *    <tr><td>number</td><td>{@link Number}<br>(depending on length and format, could be {@link Integer},
126 *       {@link Double}, {@link Float}, etc...)</td></tr>
127 *    <tr><td>boolean</td><td>{@link Boolean}</td></tr>
128 *    <tr><td>string</td><td>{@link String}</td></tr>
129 * </table>
130 *
131 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
132 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
133 * </ul>
134 *
135 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
136 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SerializersAndParsers">Serializers and Parsers</a>
137
138 * </ul>
139 */
140@SuppressWarnings({
141   "rawtypes",
142   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_autoCloseStreams)
143   "resource" // Closeable resources are owned by the caller's parser session; Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
144})
145public class Parser extends MarshallingContextable {
146
147   // Property name constants
148   private static final String PROP_autoCloseStreams = "autoCloseStreams";
149   private static final String PROP_debugOutputLines = "debugOutputLines";
150   private static final String PROP_listener = "listener";
151   private static final String PROP_nulls = "nulls";
152   private static final String PROP_trimStrings = "trimStrings";
153   private static final String PROP_unbuffered = "unbuffered";
154
155   /**
156    * Builder class.
157    */
158   @SuppressWarnings({
159      "java:S119" // 'SELF' (CRTP self-type) is intentional and clearer than a single-letter name.
160   })
161   public abstract static class Builder<SELF extends Builder<SELF>> extends MarshallingContextable.Builder<SELF> {
162
163      private boolean autoCloseStreams;
164      private boolean trimStrings;
165      private boolean unbuffered;
166      private Class<? extends ParserListener> listener;
167      private int debugOutputLines;
168      private String consumes;
169      private Nulls nulls;
170
171      /**
172       * Constructor, default settings.
173       */
174      protected Builder() {
175         autoCloseStreams = env("Parser.autoCloseStreams", false);
176         trimStrings = env("Parser.trimStrings", false);
177         unbuffered = env("Parser.unbuffered", false);
178         debugOutputLines = env("Parser.debugOutputLines", 5);
179         listener = null;
180         consumes = null;
181         nulls = Nulls.NOT_SET;
182      }
183
184      /**
185       * Copy constructor.
186       *
187       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
188       */
189      protected Builder(Builder<?> copyFrom) {
190         super(copyFrom);
191         autoCloseStreams = copyFrom.autoCloseStreams;
192         trimStrings = copyFrom.trimStrings;
193         unbuffered = copyFrom.unbuffered;
194         debugOutputLines = copyFrom.debugOutputLines;
195         listener = copyFrom.listener;
196         consumes = copyFrom.consumes;
197         nulls = copyFrom.nulls;
198      }
199
200      /**
201       * Copy constructor.
202       *
203       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
204       */
205      protected Builder(Parser copyFrom) {
206         super(copyFrom);
207         autoCloseStreams = copyFrom.autoCloseStreams;
208         trimStrings = copyFrom.trimStrings;
209         unbuffered = copyFrom.unbuffered;
210         debugOutputLines = copyFrom.debugOutputLines;
211         listener = copyFrom.listener;
212         consumes = copyFrom.consumes;
213         nulls = copyFrom.nulls;
214      }
215
216      /**
217       * Auto-close streams.
218       *
219       * <p>
220       * When enabled, <l>InputStreams</l> and <l>Readers</l> passed into parsers will be closed
221       * after parsing is complete.
222       *
223       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
224       * <p class='bjava'>
225       *    <jc>// Create a parser using strict mode.</jc>
226       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
227       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
228       *       .autoCloseStreams()
229       *       .build();
230       *
231       *    Reader <jv>myReader</jv> = <jk>new</jk> FileReader(<js>"/tmp/myfile.json"</js>);
232       *    MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>myReader</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
233       *
234       *    <jsm>assertTrue</jsm>(<jv>myReader</jv>.isClosed());
235       * </p>
236       *
237       * @return This object.
238       */
239      public SELF autoCloseStreams() {
240         return autoCloseStreams(true);
241      }
242
243      /**
244       * Same as {@link #autoCloseStreams()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
245       *
246       * @param value The value for this setting.
247       * @return This object.
248       */
249      public SELF autoCloseStreams(boolean value) {
250         autoCloseStreams = value;
251         return self();
252      }
253
254      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
255      public Parser build() {
256         return build(Parser.class);
257      }
258
259      /**
260       * Specifies the media type that this parser consumes.
261       *
262       * <p>
263       * <b>Token streams:</b> this setting is not honored by {@link TokenReadable#parseTokens(Object) parseTokens}
264       * &mdash; media-type metadata is informational, not behavioral.
265       *
266       * @param value The value for this setting.
267       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk> (treated as empty string, no media types will be specified).
268       * @return This object.
269       */
270      public SELF consumes(String value) {
271         consumes = value;
272         return self();
273      }
274
275      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
276      public abstract SELF copy();
277
278      /**
279       * Debug output lines.
280       *
281       * <p>
282       * When parse errors occur, this specifies the number of lines of input before and after the
283       * error location to be printed as part of the exception message.
284       *
285       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
286       * <p class='bjava'>
287       *    <jc>// Create a parser whose exceptions print out 100 lines before and after the parse error location.</jc>
288       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
289       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
290       *       .debug()  <jc>// Enable debug mode to capture Reader contents as strings.</jc>
291       *       .debugOuputLines(100)
292       *       .build();
293       *
294       *    Reader <jv>myReader</jv> = <jk>new</jk> FileReader(<js>"/tmp/mybadfile.json"</js>);
295       *    <jk>try</jk> {
296       *       <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>myReader</jv>, Object.<jk>class</jk>);
297       *    } <jk>catch</jk> (ParseException <jv>e</jv>) {
298       *       System.<jsf>err</jsf>.println(<jv>e</jv>.getMessage());  <jc>// Will display 200 lines of the output.</jc>
299       *    }
300       * </p>
301       *
302       * <p>
303       * <b>Token streams:</b> this setting is not honored by {@link TokenReadable#parseTokens(Object) parseTokens}
304       * &mdash; it controls error-message formatting on the POJO databind path; the cursor uses its own
305       * exception text.
306       *
307       * @param value
308       *    The new value for this property.
309       *    <br>The default value is <c>5</c>.
310       * @return This object.
311       */
312      public SELF debugOutputLines(int value) {
313         debugOutputLines = value;
314         return self();
315      }
316
317      /**
318       * Returns the current value for the 'consumes' property.
319       *
320       * @return The current value for the 'consumes' property.
321       */
322      public String getConsumes() { return consumes; }
323
324      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
325      public HashKey hashKey() {
326         // @formatter:off
327         return HashKey.of(
328            super.hashKey(),
329            autoCloseStreams,
330            trimStrings,
331            unbuffered,
332            debugOutputLines,
333            listener,
334            consumes,
335            nulls
336         );
337         // @formatter:on
338      }
339
340      /**
341       * Parser listener.
342       *
343       * <p>
344       * Class used to listen for errors and warnings that occur during parsing.
345       *
346       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
347       * <p class='bjava'>
348       *    <jc>// Define our parser listener.</jc>
349       *    <jc>// Simply captures all unknown bean property events.</jc>
350       *    <jk>public class</jk> MyParserListener <jk>extends</jk> ParserListener {
351       *
352       *       <jc>// A simple property to store our events.</jc>
353       *       <jk>public</jk> List&lt;String&gt; <jf>events</jf> = <jk>new</jk> LinkedList&lt;&gt;();
354       *
355       *       <ja>@Override</ja>
356       *       <jk>public</jk> &lt;T&gt; <jk>void</jk> onUnknownBeanProperty(ParserSession <jv>session</jv>, String <jv>propertyName</jv>, Class&lt;T&gt; <jv>beanClass</jv>, T <jv>bean</jv>) {
357       *          Position <jv>position</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.getPosition();
358       *          <jf>events</jf>.add(<jv>propertyName</jv> + <js>","</js> + <jv>position</jv>.getLine() + <js>","</js> + <jv>position</jv>.getColumn());
359       *       }
360       *    }
361       *
362       *    <jc>// Create a parser using our listener.</jc>
363       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
364       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
365       *       .listener(MyParserListener.<jk>class</jk>)
366       *       .build();
367       *
368       *    <jc>// Create a session object.</jc>
369       *    <jc>// Needed because listeners are created per-session.</jc>
370       *    <jk>try</jk> (ReaderParserSession <jv>session</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.createSession()) {
371       *
372       *       <jc>// Parse some JSON object.</jc>
373       *       MyBean <jv>myBean</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.parse(<js>"{...}"</js>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
374       *
375       *       <jc>// Get the listener.</jc>
376       *       MyParserListener <jv>listener</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.getListener(MyParserListener.<jk>class</jk>);
377       *
378       *       <jc>// Dump the results to the console.</jc>
379       *       Json5.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>.println(<jv>listener</jv>.<jf>events</jf>);
380       *    }
381       * </p>
382       *
383       * <p>
384       * <b>Token streams:</b> this setting is not honored by {@link TokenReadable#parseTokens(Object) parseTokens}
385       * &mdash; listeners are POJO-level observers with no token-layer analog.
386       *
387       * @param value The new value for this property.
388       *    <br>Can be <jk>null</jk>.
389       * @return This object.
390       */
391      public SELF listener(Class<? extends ParserListener> value) {
392         listener = value;
393         return self();
394      }
395
396      /**
397       * Sets the default per-property null-coercion policy applied during parsing.
398       *
399       * <p>
400       * Juneau's analog of Jackson's
401       * <c>ObjectMapper.setDefaultSetterInfo(JsonSetter.Value.forValueNulls(...))</c>.
402       * Applied as the fallback when no per-property
403       * {@link MarshalledProp#nulls() @MarshalledProp(nulls=…)} is configured.
404       *
405       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
406       * <p class='bjava'>
407       *    <jc>// Substitute the empty value for any property whose wire value is null.</jc>
408       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
409       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
410       *       .nulls(Nulls.<jsf>EMPTY</jsf>)
411       *       .build();
412       * </p>
413       *
414       * @param value The new value for this property.
415       *    <br>{@code null} or {@link Nulls#NOT_SET} disables the default (each property falls back to
416       *    {@link Nulls#LEAVE}).
417       * @return This object.
418       * @since 10.0.0
419       */
420      public SELF nulls(Nulls value) {
421         nulls = value == null ? Nulls.NOT_SET : value;
422         return self();
423      }
424
425      /**
426       * Returns the current context-level {@link Nulls} default configured on this builder.
427       *
428       * @return The current value (never <jk>null</jk>; {@link Nulls#NOT_SET} when unconfigured).
429       * @since 10.0.0
430       */
431      public Nulls getNulls() { return nulls == null ? Nulls.NOT_SET : nulls; }
432
433      /**
434       * Trim parsed strings.
435       *
436       * <p>
437       * When enabled, string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being added to
438       * the POJO.
439       *
440       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
441       * <p class='bjava'>
442       *    <jc>// Create a parser with trim-strings enabled.</jc>
443       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser
444       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
445       *       .trimStrings()
446       *       .build();
447       *
448       *    <jc>// Use it.</jc>
449       *    String <jv>json</jv> = <js>"{' foo ':' bar '}"</js>;
450       *    Map&lt;String,String&gt; <jv>myMap</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, HashMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
451       *
452       *    <jc>// Make sure strings are parsed.</jc>
453       *    <jsm>assertEquals</jsm>(<js>"bar"</js>, <jv>myMap</jv>.get(<js>"foo"</js>));
454       * </p>
455       *
456       * @return This object.
457       */
458      public SELF trimStrings() {
459         return trimStrings(true);
460      }
461
462      /**
463       * Same as {@link #trimStrings()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
464       *
465       * @param value The value for this setting.
466       * @return This object.
467       */
468      public SELF trimStrings(boolean value) {
469         trimStrings = value;
470         return self();
471      }
472
473      /**
474       * Unbuffered.
475       *
476       * <p>
477       * When enabled, don't use internal buffering during parsing.
478       *
479       * <p>
480       * This is useful in cases when you want to parse the same input stream or reader multiple times
481       * because it may contain multiple independent POJOs to parse.
482       * <br>Buffering would cause the parser to read past the current POJO in the stream.
483       *
484       * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
485       * <p class='bjava'>
486       *    <jc>// Create a parser using strict mode.</jc>
487       *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.
488       *       .<jsm>create</jsm>()
489       *       .unbuffered(<jk>true</jk>)
490       *       .build();
491       *
492       *    <jc>// If you're calling parse on the same input multiple times, use a session instead of the parser directly.</jc>
493       *    <jc>// It's more efficient because we don't need to recalc the session settings again. </jc>
494       *    ReaderParserSession <jv>session</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.createSession();
495       *
496       *    <jc>// Read input with multiple POJOs</jc>
497       *    Reader <jv>json</jv> = <jk>new</jk> StringReader(<js>"{foo:'bar'}{foo:'baz'}"</js>);
498       *    MyBean <jv>myBean1</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
499       *    MyBean <jv>myBean2</jv> = <jv>session</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
500       * </p>
501       *
502       * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
503       *    <li class='note'>
504       *       This only allows for multi-input streams for the following parsers:
505       *       <ul>
506       *          <li class='jc'>{@link JsonParser}
507       *          <li class='jc'>{@link UonParser}
508       *       </ul>
509       *       It has no effect on the following parsers:
510       *       <ul>
511       *          <li class='jc'>{@link MsgPackParser} - It already doesn't use buffering.
512       *          <li class='jc'>{@link XmlParser}, {@link HtmlParser} - These use StAX which doesn't allow for more than one root element anyway.
513       *          <li>RDF parsers - These read everything into an internal model before any parsing begins.
514       *       </ul>
515       * </ul>
516       *
517       * @return This object.
518       */
519      public SELF unbuffered() {
520         return unbuffered(true);
521      }
522
523      /**
524       * Same as {@link #unbuffered()} but allows you to explicitly specify the value.
525       *
526       * @param value The value for this setting.
527       * @return This object.
528       */
529      public SELF unbuffered(boolean value) {
530         unbuffered = value;
531         return self();
532      }
533
534
535   }
536
537   /**
538    * Concrete default builder leaf for the non-subclassed {@link Parser#create()} / {@link Parser#copy()} path.
539    */
540   public static final class DefaultBuilder extends Builder<DefaultBuilder> {
541
542      DefaultBuilder() {}
543
544      DefaultBuilder(Parser copyFrom) {
545         super(copyFrom);
546      }
547
548      DefaultBuilder(Builder<?> copyFrom) {
549         super(copyFrom);
550      }
551
552      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
553      public DefaultBuilder copy() {
554         return new DefaultBuilder(this);
555      }
556   }
557
558   /**
559    * Represents no Parser.
560    */
561   public abstract static class Null extends Parser {
562      @SuppressWarnings({
563         "java:S1186" // Constructor required by Parser parent class, even though Null is abstract and never instantiated directly
564      })
565      private Null(Builder<?> builder) {
566         super(builder);
567      }
568   }
569
570   /**
571    * Creates a new builder for this object.
572    *
573    * @return A new builder.
574    */
575   @SuppressWarnings({
576      "java:S1452" // Self-typed builder: Builder<?> is the only non-raw, leaf-free return; chaining + build() unaffected.
577   })
578   public static Builder<?> create() {
579      return new DefaultBuilder();
580   }
581
582   /**
583    * Instantiates a builder of the specified parser class.
584    *
585    * <p>
586    * Looks for a public static method called <c>create</c> that returns an object that can be passed into a public
587    * or protected constructor of the class.
588    *
589    * @param c The builder to create.
590    * @return A new builder.
591    */
592   public static Builder createParserBuilder(Class<? extends Parser> c) {
593      return (Builder)Context.createBuilder(c);
594   }
595
596   protected final boolean autoCloseStreams;
597   protected final boolean trimStrings;
598   protected final boolean unbuffered;
599   protected final int debugOutputLines;
600   protected final Class<? extends ParserListener> listener;
601   protected final String consumes;
602   protected final Nulls nulls;
603   private final List<MediaType> consumesArray;
604
605   /**
606    * Constructor.
607    *
608    * @param builder The builder this object.
609    */
610   protected Parser(Builder<?> builder) {
611      super(builder);
612
613      autoCloseStreams = builder.autoCloseStreams;
614      consumes = builder.consumes;
615      debugOutputLines = builder.debugOutputLines;
616      listener = builder.listener;
617      trimStrings = builder.trimStrings;
618      unbuffered = builder.unbuffered;
619      nulls = builder.nulls == null ? Nulls.NOT_SET : builder.nulls;
620
621   String[] consumesParts = splita(nn(consumes) ? consumes : "");
622   List<MediaType> consumesList = new ArrayList<>();
623   for (var consume : consumesParts) {
624      consumesList.add(MediaType.of(consume));
625   }
626   this.consumesArray = u(consumesList);
627   }
628
629   /**
630    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parser can handle the specified content type.
631    *
632    * @param contentType The content type to test.
633    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parser can handle the specified content type.
634    */
635   public boolean canHandle(String contentType) {
636      if (nn(contentType))
637         for (var mt : getMediaTypes())
638            if (contentType.equals(mt.toString()))
639               return true;
640      return false;
641   }
642
643   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
644   public Builder<?> copy() {
645      return new DefaultBuilder(this);
646   }
647
648   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
649   public ParserSession.Builder<?> createSession() {
650      return ParserSession.create(this);
651   }
652
653   /**
654    * Workhorse method.
655    *
656    * <p>
657    * Subclasses are expected to either implement this method or {@link ParserSession#doParse(ParserPipe, ClassMeta)}.
658    *
659    * @param session The current session.
660    * @param pipe Where to get the input from.
661    * @param type
662    *    The class type of the object to create.
663    *    If <jk>null</jk> or <code>Object.<jk>class</jk></code>, object type is based on what's being parsed.
664    *    For example, when parsing JSON text, it may return a <c>String</c>, <c>Number</c>,
665    *    <c>JsonMap</c>, etc...
666    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
667    * @return The parsed object.
668    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
669    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
670    * @throws ExecutableException Exception occurred on invoked constructor/method/field.
671    */
672   public <T> T doParse(ParserSession session, ParserPipe pipe, ClassMeta<T> type) throws IOException, ParseException {
673      throw uoex();
674   }
675
676   /**
677    * Returns the media types handled based on the values passed to the <c>consumes</c> constructor parameter.
678    *
679    * @return The list of media types.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
680    */
681   public final List<MediaType> getMediaTypes() { return consumesArray; }
682
683   /**
684    * Returns the first media type handled based on the values passed to the <c>consumes</c> constructor parameter.
685    *
686    * @return The media type.
687    */
688   public final MediaType getPrimaryMediaType() { return first(consumesArray).orElse(null); }
689
690   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
691   public ParserSession getSession() { return createSession().build(); }
692
693   /**
694    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this parser subclasses from {@link ReaderParser}.
695    *
696    * @return <jk>true</jk> if this parser subclasses from {@link ReaderParser}.
697    */
698   public boolean isReaderParser() { return true; }
699
700   /**
701    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} except optimized for a non-parameterized class.
702    *
703    * <p>
704    * This is the preferred parse method for simple types since you don't need to cast the results.
705    *
706    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
707    * <p class='bjava'>
708    *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
709    *
710    *    <jc>// Parse into a string.</jc>
711    *    String <jv>string</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
712    *
713    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean.</jc>
714    *    MyBean <jv>bean</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
715    *
716    *    <jc>// Parse into a bean array.</jc>
717    *    MyBean[] <jv>beanArray</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, MyBean[].<jk>class</jk>);
718    *
719    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of objects.</jc>
720    *    List <jv>list</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>);
721    *
722    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of object keys/values.</jc>
723    *    Map <jv>map</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>);
724    * </p>
725    *
726    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
727    * @param input
728    *    The input.
729    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
730    * @param type The object type to create.
731    * @return The parsed object.
732    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
733    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
734    */
735   public final <T> T parse(Object input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
736      return getSession().parse(input, type);
737   }
738
739   /**
740    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} except the type has already been converted into a {@link ClassMeta}
741    * object.
742    *
743    * <p>
744    * This is mostly an internal method used by the framework.
745    *
746    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
747    * @param input
748    *    The input.
749    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
750    * @param type The object type to create.
751    * @return The parsed object.
752    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
753    * @throws IOException Thrown by the underlying stream.
754    */
755   public final <T> T parse(Object input, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException, IOException {
756      return getSession().parse(input, type);
757   }
758
759   /**
760    * Parses input into the specified object type.
761    *
762    * <p>
763    * The type can be a simple type (e.g. beans, strings, numbers) or parameterized type (collections/maps).
764    *
765    * <h5 class='section'>Examples:</h5>
766    * <p class='bjava'>
767    *    ReaderParser <jv>parser</jv> = JsonParser.<jsf>DEFAULT</jsf>;
768    *
769    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of strings.</jc>
770    *    List <jv>list1</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
771    *
772    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of beans.</jc>
773    *    List <jv>list2</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
774    *
775    *    <jc>// Parse into a linked-list of linked-lists of strings.</jc>
776    *    List <jv>list3</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, LinkedList.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
777    *
778    *    <jc>// Parse into a map of string keys/values.</jc>
779    *    Map <jv>map1</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>);
780    *
781    *    <jc>// Parse into a map containing string keys and values of lists containing beans.</jc>
782    *    Map <jv>map2</jv> = <jv>parser</jv>.parse(<jv>json</jv>, TreeMap.<jk>class</jk>, String.<jk>class</jk>, List.<jk>class</jk>, MyBean.<jk>class</jk>);
783    * </p>
784    *
785    * <p>
786    * <c>Collection</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or one objects indicating the element type.
787    *
788    * <p>
789    * <c>Map</c> classes are assumed to be followed by zero or two meta objects indicating the key and value types.
790    *
791    * <p>
792    * The array can be arbitrarily long to indicate arbitrarily complex data structures.
793    *
794    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
795    *    <li class='note'>
796    *       Use the {@link #parse(Object, Class)} method instead if you don't need a parameterized map/collection.
797    * </ul>
798    *
799    * @param <T> The class type of the object to create.
800    * @param input
801    *    The input.
802    *    <br>Character-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
803    *    <ul>
804    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
805    *       <li>{@link Reader}
806    *       <li>{@link CharSequence}
807    *       <li>{@link InputStream} containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
808    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
809    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code> containing UTF-8 encoded text (or charset defined by
810    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#streamCharset(Charset)} property value).
811    *       <li>{@link File} containing system encoded text (or charset defined by
812    *          {@link ReaderParser.Builder#fileCharset(Charset)} property value).
813    *    </ul>
814    *    <br>Stream-based parsers can handle the following input class types:
815    *    <ul>
816    *       <li><jk>null</jk>
817    *       <li>{@link InputStream}
818    *       <li><code><jk>byte</jk>[]</code>
819    *       <li>{@link File}
820    *       <li>{@link CharSequence} containing encoded bytes according to the {@link InputStreamParser.Builder#binaryFormat(BinaryFormat)} setting.
821    *    </ul>
822    * @param type
823    *    The object type to create.
824    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
825    * @param args
826    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
827    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
828    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
829    * @return The parsed object.
830    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
831    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
832    * @see MarshallingSession#getClassMeta(Type,Type...) for argument syntax for maps and collections.
833    */
834   public final <T> T parse(Object input, Type type, Type...args) throws ParseException, IOException {
835      return getSession().parse(input, type, args);
836   }
837
838   /**
839    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Class)} but since it's a {@link String} input doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
840    *
841    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
842    * @param input
843    *    The input.
844    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
845    * @param type The object type to create.
846    * @return The parsed object.
847    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
848    */
849   public final <T> T parse(String input, Class<T> type) throws ParseException {
850      return getSession().parse(input, type);
851   }
852
853   /**
854    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} but since it's a {@link String} input doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
855    *
856    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
857    * @param input
858    *    The input.
859    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
860    * @param type The object type to create.
861    * @return The parsed object.
862    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
863    */
864   public final <T> T parse(String input, ClassMeta<T> type) throws ParseException {
865      return getSession().parse(input, type);
866   }
867
868   /**
869    * Same as {@link #parse(String, ClassMeta)} but creates the parser session with a specific outer object.
870    *
871    * <p>
872    * The outer object is used when instantiating top-level non-static inner classes and when setting
873    * {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty} fields on parsed beans.
874    * This is important when a parser is used to parse inline content (e.g. JSON5 embedded in a Markdown
875    * table cell) where the parent bean context must be preserved.
876    *
877    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
878    * @param input The input string to parse.
879    * @param type The object type to create.
880    * @param outer
881    *    The outer object to associate with this parse session.
882    *    Used for {@link ParentProperty @ParentProperty} injection and
883    *    non-static inner class construction.
884    *    Can be <jk>null</jk>.
885    * @return The parsed object.
886    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
887    */
888   public final <T> T parseWithOuter(String input, ClassMeta<T> type, Object outer) throws ParseException {
889      return createSession().outer(outer).build().parse(input, type);
890   }
891
892   /**
893    * Same as {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} but since it's a {@link String} input doesn't throw an {@link IOException}.
894    *
895    * @param <T> The class type of the object being created.
896    * @param input
897    *    The input.
898    *    See {@link #parse(Object, Type, Type...)} for details.
899    * @param type
900    *    The object type to create.
901    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
902    * @param args
903    *    The type arguments of the class if it's a collection or map.
904    *    <br>Can be any of the following: {@link ClassMeta}, {@link Class}, {@link ParameterizedType}, {@link GenericArrayType}
905    *    <br>Ignored if the main type is not a map or collection.
906    * @return The parsed object.
907    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
908    */
909   public final <T> T parse(String input, Type type, Type...args) throws ParseException {
910      return getSession().parse(input, type, args);
911   }
912
913   /**
914    * Parses the specified array input with each entry in the object defined by the {@code argTypes}
915    * argument.
916    *
917    * <p>
918    * Used for converting arrays (e.g. <js>"[arg1,arg2,...]"</js>) into an {@code Object[]} that can be passed
919    * to the {@code Method.invoke(target, args)} method.
920    *
921    * <p>
922    * Used in the following locations:
923    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
924    *    <li>
925    *       Used to parse argument strings in the {@link ObjectIntrospector#invokeMethod(Method, Reader)} method.
926    * </ul>
927    *
928    * @param input The input.  Subclasses can support different input types.
929    * @param argTypes Specifies the type of objects to create for each entry in the array.
930    * @return An array of parsed objects.
931    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
932    */
933   public final Object[] parseArgs(Object input, Type[] argTypes) throws ParseException {
934      if (argTypes == null || argTypes.length == 0)
935         return new Object[0];
936      return getSession().parseArgs(input, argTypes);
937   }
938
939   /**
940    * Parses the contents of the specified reader and loads the results into the specified collection.
941    *
942    * <p>
943    * Used in the following locations:
944    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
945    *    <li>
946    *       The various character-based constructors in {@link JsonList} (e.g.
947    *       {@link JsonList#JsonList(CharSequence,Parser)}.
948    * </ul>
949    *
950    * @param <E> The element class type.
951    * @param input The input.  See {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} for supported input types.
952    * @param c The collection being loaded.
953    * @param elementType The class type of the elements, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
954    * @return The same collection that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
955    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
956    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If not implemented.
957    */
958   public final <E> Collection<E> parseIntoCollection(Object input, Collection<E> c, Type elementType) throws ParseException {
959      return getSession().parseIntoCollection(input, c, elementType);
960   }
961
962   /**
963    * Parses the contents of the specified reader and loads the results into the specified map.
964    *
965    * <p>
966    * Reader must contain something that serializes to a map (such as text containing a JSON object).
967    *
968    * <p>
969    * Used in the following locations:
970    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
971    *    <li>
972    *       The various character-based constructors in {@link JsonMap} (e.g.
973    *       {@link JsonMap#JsonMap(CharSequence,Parser)}).
974    * </ul>
975    *
976    * @param <K> The key class type.
977    * @param <V> The value class type.
978    * @param input The input.  See {@link #parse(Object, ClassMeta)} for supported input types.
979    * @param m The map being loaded.
980    * @param keyType The class type of the keys, or <jk>null</jk> to default to <code>String.<jk>class</jk></code>.
981    * @param valueType The class type of the values, or <jk>null</jk> to default to whatever is being parsed.
982    * @return The same map that was passed in to allow this method to be chained.
983    * @throws ParseException Malformed input encountered.
984    * @throws UnsupportedOperationException If not implemented.
985    */
986   public final <K,V> Map<K,V> parseIntoMap(Object input, Map<K,V> m, Type keyType, Type valueType) throws ParseException {
987      return getSession().parseIntoMap(input, m, keyType, valueType);
988   }
989
990   /**
991    * Debug output lines.
992    *
993    * @see Parser.Builder#debugOutputLines(int)
994    * @return
995    *    The number of lines of input before and after the error location to be printed as part of the exception message.
996    */
997   protected final int getDebugOutputLines() { return debugOutputLines; }
998
999   /**
1000    * Parser listener.
1001    *
1002    * @see Parser.Builder#listener(Class)
1003    * @return
1004    *    Class used to listen for errors and warnings that occur during parsing.
1005    */
1006   protected final Class<? extends ParserListener> getListener() { return listener; }
1007
1008   /**
1009    * Auto-close streams.
1010    *
1011    * @see Parser.Builder#autoCloseStreams()
1012    * @return
1013    *    <jk>true</jk> if <l>InputStreams</l> and <l>Readers</l> passed into parsers will be closed
1014    *    after parsing is complete.
1015    */
1016   protected final boolean isAutoCloseStreams() { return autoCloseStreams; }
1017
1018   /**
1019    * Trim parsed strings.
1020    *
1021    * @see Parser.Builder#trimStrings()
1022    * @return
1023    *    <jk>true</jk> if string values will be trimmed of whitespace using {@link String#trim()} before being added to
1024    *    the POJO.
1025    */
1026   protected final boolean isTrimStrings() { return trimStrings; }
1027
1028   /**
1029    * Returns the context-level {@link Nulls} default consulted at parse-time when no per-property
1030    * {@link MarshalledProp#nulls() @MarshalledProp(nulls=…)} is configured.
1031    *
1032    * @return The current value (never <jk>null</jk>; {@link Nulls#NOT_SET} when unconfigured).
1033    * @see Parser.Builder#nulls(Nulls)
1034    * @since 10.0.0
1035    */
1036   public final Nulls getNulls() { return nulls; }
1037
1038   /**
1039    * Unbuffered.
1040    *
1041    * @see Parser.Builder#unbuffered()
1042    * @return
1043    *    <jk>true</jk> if parsers don't use internal buffering during parsing.
1044    */
1045   protected final boolean isUnbuffered() { return unbuffered; }
1046
1047   @Override /* Overridden from MarshallingContextable */
1048   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
1049      return super.properties()
1050         .a(PROP_autoCloseStreams, autoCloseStreams)
1051         .a(PROP_debugOutputLines, debugOutputLines)
1052         .a(PROP_listener, listener)
1053         .a(PROP_nulls, nulls)
1054         .a(PROP_trimStrings, trimStrings)
1055         .a(PROP_unbuffered, unbuffered);
1056   }
1057}
